implements: YZXCONV-021 root coverage (spine ARCHBP-021), executing the ARCHBP-134 hybrid strategy

toolchain.cc: flexnetosMuslToolchain (pkgsCross.musl64 stdenv.cc via the yazelix flake, profile-exposed as x86_64-linux-musl-gcc)
toolchain.build_discipline: cargo build --release --locked, two fully independent CARGO_TARGET_DIR trees under /home/flexnetos/meta/var/cache/cargo-target (disk-backed, never tmpfs), byte-hash compared

musl_artifacts.name: yzx-open
musl_artifacts.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, static-pie linked

musl_artifacts.name: yzx-tutor
musl_artifacts.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, static-pie linked

musl_artifacts.name: yzx-yazi-config
musl_artifacts.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, static-pie linked

non_musl_classification.fallback: relocatable extracted closure inside the envelope; musl-static WebKitGTK is not practical and forcing it would drop the Glass front door — a forbidden downgrade
closure-shipped server binaries with the durable datadir bound via --durable; the datadir itself never ships inside a bundle

non_musl_classification.dependency: zellij / nushell / helix / yazi TUI stack
non_musl_classification.fallback: relocatable extracted closure; upstream musl builds exist but rebuilding third-party binaries breaks the proven-source supply-chain rule, so the closure carries the exact tested store paths
relocatable extracted closure; upstream ships glibc builds only, closure travel keeps the tested byte

musl-eligible once its peer path-deps are vendored or built from the main checkout where ../../../ratconfig resolves; excluded from this artifact set rather than force-built from the isolated worktree

recorded chain bwrap(nix-store) -> bwrap(AppArmor-profiled host /usr/bin/bwrap) -> proot; on this host class the store bwrap is denied by kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=1 and the profiled host bwrap carries the launch (honest downgrade, ARCHBP-065)

closure.extraction: nix-store -qR over the release roots, copied byte-preserving into <bundle>/nixroot/store

relocation.launcher: bundle-root copy of yzx-envelope.sh with the new --store option: binds <bundle>/nixroot at /nix, hiding the host store entirely
relocation.test_a_store_bound: bundle bash executed from the moved prefix with the host store hidden: printed relocation-ok and ls /nix/store showed exactly 20 paths (the bundle closure; the host store holds thousands) — glibc interpreter and libraries resolved from the bundle alone
relocation.test_b_store_free: musl yzx-tutor executed with a completely EMPTY /nix (exit 0) — the static-pie artifact needs no store at all

remaining: launcher bootstrap: the envelope script itself runs under a host bash and a host (or profiled) bwrap on AppArmor-restricted hosts — the bundle does not yet self-bootstrap from a bare kernel
development continues against the shared host /nix/store (tier map: nix-profile-frontdoor is portable-tier only at release)

the full-stack release roots (zellij/nushell/GUI/PostgreSQL) are not yet closure-extracted — this receipt proves the mechanism on the envelope+bash root set; T10 gauntlets (yzx-iso t10-3/t10-4) extend it to the full stack
host_nix_dependence.claim_boundary: No universal no-/nix/store claim is made: only the three musl artifacts are store-free; everything else travels as a closure that the envelope re-binds at a private /nix.
